mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near zero reduces the risk-free discount rate and corporate borrowing costs, which increases the present value of future equity cash flows and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the average 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% is negative over any 3-year rolling window, or if the Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above six percent signals excess labor market slack that depresses wage growth and consumer spending power, leading to downward revisions in corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: This hypothesis fails if the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than fifty percent of triggered months when UNRATE exceeds six percent over a five-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales reveal a coordinated contraction in business and household demand that amplifies recession risk beyond either single indicator, producing negative S&P 500 quarterly returns.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the joint decline signal does not produce a lower average 60-day return than either INDPRO or RSAFS alone over a five-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during post-recession recovery periods signals accelerating capacity utilization and operating leverage that boosts corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if positive 5-day returns occur in less than fifty-five percent of months with rising INDPRO during post-recession recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below twenty-five basis points reflects ultra-accommodative monetary policy that suppresses short-term real yields and incentivizes risk-taking, producing positive S&P 500 quarterly returns.
failure_condition: This hypothesis is rejected if the average 60-day forward return is negative when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales establishes a self-reinforcing income-consumption loop that boosts top-line revenue growth for listed companies, producing positive S&P 500 monthly returns larger than either series predicts independently.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if the joint acceleration signal does not achieve a higher average 20-day return than either PAYEMS or RSAFS alone over a five-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Momentum in retail sales directly translates into near-term revenue surprises for the consumer-facing constituents of the S&P 500, so rising retail sales predict positive index returns over the next week.
failure_condition: This hypothesis is rejected if the average 5-day forward return when RSAFS is rising is not positive over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal impending inventory overhang and margin compression, triggering earnings downgrades and negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if the S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than fifty percent of instances triggered by two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO during late-cycle expansions.
